key_points: West Apartments has documented, recurring mold problem since at least 2019
Dorm furniture unchanged since 1970s; accordion closet doors widely mocked
Bathroom hygiene in some dorms is genuinely disturbing per residents
key_quotes: I was forced to change apartments 2 times due to mold growth in less than 2 months. I got three UTIs back to back within a month. Pissing blood for a MONTH.
I went to SBU in 2008 and it still looks the same.
The closet covers ARE HORRENDOUS- where the heck did SBU even find those horrid plastic sliding accordion doors from 1976

key_points: SBU is widely acknowledged as a commuter/low social-scene school
Campus culture described as 'gray, repetitive, and kind of lifeless'
Pockets of genuine community exist but require active seeking out
key_quotes: SBU does not have a college culture. It has a business culture with students as population.
I heard SBU isn't too fun.
Place has weird vibes.

key_points: Meal plan costs $2,775 to load $2,600 — students pay $175 just to use their own money
Students burn through $2,600 dining dollars faster than expected
Food trucks accused of being overpriced; one student literally caught a squirrel
key_quotes: It costs $2775 to put $2600 into your account.
I spent my last $4.50 on a Dunkin' chai latte which wasn't even that good and got a notification saying I had run out of dining dollars.
All the food trucks here are overpriced slop anyway.

key_points: Multiple student suicides on campus within a single academic year
Admin routinely criticized for ignoring student deaths and mental health crises
CAPS and TimelyCare reported as inadequate; students posting crisis hotlines on Reddit themselves
key_quotes: This is the second person who did it this year. I hope they at least acknowledge this one.
I tried to use TimelyCare for therapy a few years back and on-campus resources only go so far.
In light of recent events, a lot of SBU just feels gray, repetitive, and kind of lifeless.

key_points: In-state tuition is genuinely affordable; merit scholarships available for strong applicants
Out-of-state students can receive significant presidential scholarships reducing cost substantially
Financial aid amounts can drop unexpectedly year-to-year with little explanation
key_quotes: I got the presidential scholarship. I pay $15k year vs. $34-$35k.
Compared to last year, I was getting remarkably less Pell Grant and TAP money.
Food should be subsidized, idc what y'all say. ITS A STATE SCHOOL FOR GODS SAKE.

key_points: Grade deflation is real and widely reported, especially in STEM prereqs
CS and STEM programs nationally ranked and respected; strong research output
Class registration is a nightmare — students routinely locked out of required courses
key_quotes: Registering for BIO 204 was like a labor of Hercules.
Not sure why they don't offer enough classes so everyone can enroll.
Tests are like 80% of the grade wth.

key_points: Admin called 150 police officers to arrest peaceful protesters; charges later dropped
Google accounts deleted days after graduation despite promised 200-day grace period
Blizzard cancellation decisions routinely delayed despite travel bans affecting commuters
key_quotes: Your tuition dollars hard at work defending the zionist regime. Think about that next time you get waitlisted because they admitted more students than they have the capacity to provide classes for.
Somebody getting fired LOL — after Google accounts were nuked 4 days post-graduation.
They notoriously hate their commuters, and also think we all ride the train.

key_points: Getting to campus from NYC via public transit takes up to 2+ hours each way
Campus architecture is brutalist and described as 'gray' and 'lifeless' by students
Natural surroundings — deer, geese, fog, snow — are genuinely beautiful and beloved
key_quotes: I could fly to California and back in the time it takes me to get to Stony Brook from NYC.
A lot of SBU just feels gray, repetitive, and kind of lifeless. You go from dorm to class to library and it all is the same tone.
Location - not near any towns.

key_points: Job placement in tech is a real concern; YouTube video on SBU grads shows only ~10% with offers
International students face particularly brutal OPT/visa job market constraints
Strong research reputation helps grad school prospects more than industry job hunting
key_quotes: I went to Stony Brook Class of 2019 and I am still trying to find a job.
The global market in tech is a big issue now. Not even 20 percent will get an offer.
Many of my college friends studied from Stony Brook. It is one of the best universities in the world.

key_points: For in-state STEM students, SBU is among the best value public universities in New York
Hidden fees — meal plan surcharges, replacement IDs, activity fees — add up fast
Strong students regularly choose SBU over more expensive privates for grad school pipeline
key_quotes: I care more about where I get into graduate school, I'm not wasting no dollar bill on a bachelor's.
I got accepted to SBU honors with a half-tuition scholarship.
It costs $2775 to put $2600 into your account.

key_points: Grindy, STEM-heavy culture; not a school known for fun or school spirit
Genuine warmth exists in small moments — student athlete high-fives, halal truck guys, bike cover strangers
Students bond over shared suffering more than shared celebration
key_quotes: Be like Wolfie means refuse to speak to anybody, crash various events, and wear the same outfit everyday.
First post here that is actually wholesome.
It's a fairly large flagship state university with a business culture and students as population.

key_points: Mental Health: multiple suicides this year; admin silent, no class cancellations
Housing: mold in West Apartments documented since 2019, still unresolved in 2024
Administration: protesters arrested by 150 officers, charges dropped; school sued for cover-up
key_quotes: This is the second person who did it this year. I hope they at least acknowledge this one.
I was forced to change apartments 2 times due to mold growth in less than 2 months. Pissing blood for a MONTH.
They are being sued because they failed to comply with FOIL. Every admin involved with organizing the dismantling of the protest should be held accountable.

key_points: Hospital library is open 24/7 — a legitimate all-night study option most students miss
Halal truck staff are genuinely kind; fed a student on credit when they had no cash
Wang Center, Staller second floor, and outdoor areas near ESS are stunning study spots
key_quotes: Hospital Library open 24/7 — to reach: Hospital/Chapin bus or walking towards Gersten.
The guys at the truck were kind enough to trust I would pay when I came the next time and made sure I ate for the day.
Take math requirements at Suffolk Community College and have them transferred over — that way you bypass those and they do not hurt your GPA.
